preliminary_interpretation.h1_demographic_amplification: NOT SUPPORTED at smoke scale. All p-values non-significant; Cramer's V trivially small. Three of five occupations show identical gender distributions between baseline and aesthetic-filtered subsets. Caveat: high DeepFace face-detection miss rate likely (many LAION images are non-portrait), reducing effective sample further.
preliminary_interpretation.h2_aesthetic_narrowing: SUPPORTED, consistently. Color-histogram variance ratio is 0.44-0.59 across all five leadership occupations -- the aesthetic-filtered subset shows roughly half the visual variance of baseline. Robust signal at smoke scale.
preliminary_interpretation.implication_for_paper: Stage 4 of RAR localizes at the AESTHETIC layer (visual distribution narrowing) but NOT at the DEMOGRAPHIC layer (gender stereotype amplification). The framework's gender-amplification prediction needs to be revised to: 'human-rated aesthetic curation narrows the representational distribution along visual-style dimensions; demographic amplification at this specific pipeline stage is not detected at this scale.' This is the kind of falsification-with-partial-confirmation that strengthens the paper's credibility, not weakens it.
preliminary_interpretation.next_step: Run FULL paper-quality audit: SMOKE=False. n=1000 per (occupation, subset) for all 15 occupations across 3 aesthetic thresholds. ~6-8 hours on Kaggle P100. The results determine the paper's final empirical anchor.
